ops.title: Sequential implementation lanes on a shared tree: per-lane green is not integration green — the refute/verify stage must run the whole suite on the final combined tree
ops.body:
During the 2026-07-18 pack phase-2 fan-out (grep/rg/json guard migrations, sequential
sol lanes on the shared cc-context tree), every lane reported green in isolation, but
the combined tree failed CI's whole-dir pytest: the rg lane changed `rg_note(evt)` to
`rg_note(evt, pairs)` while a grep-owned test file (`test_grep_guards.py:125`) held a
stale one-arg cross-reference. The grep lane measured "134 passed" *before* the rg
lane's change landed; only a refuter running the full suite on the final tree caught it.

**Why:** lanes verify against the tree as it exists at their finish time; later lanes
invalidate earlier lanes' measurements. Cross-file references (tests importing sibling
guard modules) make file-ownership boundaries porous.

**How to apply:** any multi-lane edit of one package/directory needs a final
combined-tree verification stage (full test suite, the exact CI incantation) after ALL
lanes land — run it in the refuter/synthesis stage, not per-lane. Cross-lane signature
changes are orchestrator reconciliation work; fix the stale caller in the owning file
rather than re-dispatching a lane. Related: [[workflow-script-interpolation]],
[[mcp-json-fleet-rollout]] (lane-dir/no-run discipline).
